key_themes: Disappointing 2025 results with revenue growth offset by rising costs leading to 32% pretax profit decline
Weaker order book, 17% YOY decline in early 2026 intake, and lowered 200-250p EPS guidance amid US funding uncertainty
Short-term challenging backdrop including offshore wind weakness and delayed projects
Long-term acquisition model and fundamental drivers remain intact with cost reduction actions underway
Attractive trough valuation with CEO insider buying and some analysts seeing upside despite downgrades
summary: Judges Scientific (JDG.L) reported solid revenue growth to £145.8m for 2025 but a sharp 32% drop in pretax profit to £8.9m due to cost pressures, resulting in below-expectations results and lowered 2026 EPS guidance of 200-250p. A weaker order book and ongoing US market uncertainty (despite restored research funding) have created a cautious near-term outlook, driving significant share price weakness from 2025 highs. While some investor discussion highlights a 'perfect storm' that is now derisking the valuation with insider buying and an unchanged long-term buy-and-build strategy in scientific instruments, near-term sentiment remains pressured by the earnings miss and guidance cut.[[1]](https://www.morningstar.com/news/alliance-news/1774955843604808400/judges-scientific-profit-falls-as-costs-weigh-backdrop-challenging)[[2]](https://www.lse.co.uk/news/judges-scientific-profit-falls-as-costs-weigh-backdrop-challenging-jfauaboqymthlkg.html)[[3]](https://www.proactiveinvestors.com/companies/news/1086081/judges-scientific-slips-11-as-order-book-weakens-and-us-outlook-dims-1086081.html)
sources: 10+ recent news articles (Yahoo Finance, Proactive Investors, LSE RNS, Morningstar, Simply Wall St, MarketBeat), ~5-8 relevant X posts and investor discussions (primarily from analysts and UK small-cap investors)
